title: Claude for Seniors
subtitle: A warm, patient introduction to Claude AI — for everyday help with writing, health, and family.

modules.title: Getting to Know Claude
lessons: Claude is an AI assistant made by a company called Anthropic, and you can use it for free at claude.ai — all you need is an email address to create an account. It works like a friendly, knowledgeable pen pal: you type a question or request in plain English, and Claude writes back with a helpful, thoughtful response. There is nothing to download, and it works on any computer, tablet, or smartphone. Many people find Claude particularly easy to talk to because its responses tend to be warm, clear, and patient — even when you ask the same question more than once.

Claude is especially well suited to reading and understanding long pieces of text. If you receive a long letter from a hospital, a complicated insurance document, or a lengthy email from a solicitor, you can copy and paste it into Claude and ask: "Can you explain what this letter is asking me to do, in simple terms?" Claude will read the whole thing and give you a plain-English summary of the key points and any actions you need to take. This alone can remove a great deal of stress from dealing with official paperwork.

One thing that makes Claude trustworthy is that it is designed to be honest about what it does not know. If you ask it something it is uncertain about, it will tell you rather than making something up. Claude is also very careful about health and legal topics — it will always remind you to consult a professional for personal medical or legal decisions, rather than pretending its general information is a substitute for expert advice. This honesty makes it a genuinely safe and reliable tool for everyday use.
modules.mini_task: Go to claude.ai and create a free account with your email. Once you are in, type: "Hello Claude. I am new to AI and a little nervous. Can you suggest 3 gentle ways to start using you that would be helpful for someone my age?" Read its suggestions — they will be kind and practical.

modules.title: Claude for Health Questions and Letter Writing
lessons: Claude is an excellent companion for understanding medical information. If your GP uses terms you do not recognise, or you receive a hospital letter with technical language, you can ask Claude to explain it. For example: "My doctor mentioned I have type 2 diabetes and should monitor my HbA1c levels. Can you explain what HbA1c means and why it matters?" Claude will give you a clear, jargon-free explanation. Always remember that Claude provides general information — your GP and specialist team are the right people to advise on your personal treatment.

Writing formal letters becomes much less daunting with Claude's help. Whether you need to contact your local council about a problem in your neighbourhood, write to your bank about an error on your statement, or send a polite complaint to a company, Claude can draft the letter for you. Just describe the situation in plain language — for example: "I need to write to my energy company because they have overcharged me this month. I want to be polite but clear that I expect a refund." Claude will produce a professional, well-structured letter that you can use or adapt.

Claude can also help you write more personal communications — birthday messages, thank-you notes, condolence cards, and letters to old friends. If you find it hard to express yourself in writing, simply tell Claude the relationship and how you feel, and ask it to help you put it into words. For example: "My granddaughter is turning 18. I want to write her a meaningful birthday message that tells her how proud I am. Can you help me with some ideas?" The result will feel personal because it is based on what you tell Claude about your own feelings and your relationship.
modules.mini_task: Think of a letter or message you have been meaning to write and have not got around to. Go to claude.ai and describe the situation to Claude in your own words, then ask it to help you draft the message. You might say: "I need to write to my bank about a charge I do not recognise. Can you help me write a clear, polite letter?" Adjust the draft until it feels right, then use it.

modules.title: Claude for Family, Memory, and Personal Organisation
lessons: Claude can be a wonderful tool for preserving family memories and stories. If you would like to write down memories from your life — stories from your childhood, your family history, or special events you remember — you can tell Claude the story in your own words and ask it to help you write it up clearly and beautifully. For example: "I want to write down the story of how my husband and I met. Let me tell you the story and can you help me turn it into something I could share with my grandchildren?" The result can be printed, shared, or even turned into a small booklet.

For personal organisation, Claude can help you create simple lists, reminders, and plans. Tell it about your week and ask it to help you organise your tasks: "I have a hospital appointment on Tuesday, my granddaughter is visiting on Thursday, and I need to call the dentist and sort out my prescription before Friday. Can you help me plan the week simply?" Claude will lay it out clearly, and you can ask it to add reminders or adjust the plan. It is like having a calm, organised friend to help you think things through.

Claude can also help you stay connected with technology by explaining how things work in plain language. If you receive an email you are not sure how to respond to, a confusing instruction from your internet provider, or a form you need to fill in online, you can describe it to Claude and ask for help. Type: "I have received an email saying my password needs to be updated. It looks genuine but I am not sure — what should I do?" Claude will walk you through the safest steps to take and help you understand what is being asked of you.
modules.mini_task: Think of one memory or family story that you would like to write down — even a short one is fine. Go to claude.ai and say: "I want to write down a memory about [describe the memory briefly]. Can you ask me a few questions to help me tell the story, then help me write it up?" Claude will guide you gently through the process. Save or print the result.

final_project: Use Claude to complete three meaningful personal tasks: write a letter to someone you care about, ask Claude to explain something that has been confusing you recently, and record one family memory or story in writing. Keep a short note of how Claude helped you with each task — you can even ask Claude to help you write the note.
